data.title: Moody's Upgrades LG Electronics' Credit Outlook to Positive
data.description:
Moody's Investors Service raised LG Electronics' credit rating outlook to positive from stable on Wednesday, marking the first change in four years.

The New York-headquartered financial services firm cited the South Korean electronics products maker's strong global brand, market leadership, and diversified business as key factors behind the revised credit rating outlook, the report said.

Moody's also highlighted financial improvements at LG Display , in which LG Electronics holds a 36.72% stake, as a contributor to reduced financial risk. While LG Electronics' planned IPO of its Indian subsidiary was not factored into the outlook, Moody's noted it could further strengthen the firm's financial position, it said.

Shares of LG Electronics fell nearly 1% in recent trade on Friday while those of LG Display increased nearly 3%.

data.title: Genting Singapore's Profit Slips 5% in 2024; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
Genting Singapore's net attributable profit to shareholders decreased by 5% to SG$578.9 million for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024, from SG$611.6 million a year earlier, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

Earnings per share declined to SG$0.0479, compared with SG$0.0507 in the comparable period.

Revenue, however, increased 5% year over year to SG$2.53 billion from SG$2.42 billion, mainly due to strong performance across both gaming and non-gaming segments.

The regional leisure, hospitality and integrated resorts development specialist proposed a final dividend of SG$0.02 per share for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024.

Shares of the company were down nearly 3% in recent trading.

data.title: Lightning Minerals Starts Drilling at Brazil Lithium Project; Shares Up 8%
data.description:
Lightning Minerals started drilling at the Esperança lithium project in Brazil, targeting spodumene pegmatites discovered in November 2024, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The program, covering 2,000 meters, will test the potential of pegmatites beneath those already identified at and near the surface, the filing said.

The company is also conducting ground reconnaissance at its Caraíbas and Canabrava projects, also located in Brazil, according to the filing.

Shares rose more than 8% in afternoon trade Friday.

data.title: Wilmar International's Profit Drops 39% in H2 2024; Shares Down 4%
data.description:
Wilmar International's attributable profit for the second half of 2024 declined 39% to $590.2 million from $973.9 million a year earlier, according to a late filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

Earnings per share declined to $0.095 from $0.156 in the comparable period.

Revenue, however, was up 5.3% year over year to $36.4 billion from SG$34.6 billion, mainly due to higher contributions from India and joint ventures in Southeast Asia.

The company declared a total dividend of SG$0.16 per share for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024.

Shares of the company were down nearly 4% in recent trading.

data.title: Storage Technologies and Automation to Form Dubai Subsidiary
data.description: Storage Technologies and Automation plans to incorporate a wholly-owned subsidiary in Dubai, for which it will seek board approval on Feb. 25, according to a Thursday filing to the BSE.

data.title: Risks Growing Australia's Rate Easing Cycle Will Be Slow, Shallow — Market Talk
data.description: When the Reserve Bank of Australia cut rates by 25 basis points this week, it made it painfully clear that any further loosening of policy settings would be modest given the strong labor market. The RBA's concerns seem well founded, with new data showing that job growth accelerated sharply in January, says Abhijit Surya, a senior economist at Capital Markets. To be sure, the tight labor market hasn't kept wage growth from easing, and Surya expects that will remain the case. However, with productivity set to remain anemic, unit labor cost growth won't fall to a level consistent with the RBA's 2%-3% target anytime soon, he adds. The upshot is that risks are tilting towards rates being cut more slowly, or not as far. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

data.title: Posco Shares Surge After Korea Looks at Anti-Dumping Duties on Chinese Steel
data.description:
By Kwanwoo Jun

Posco Holdings' shares rose sharply, leading a broad rally in South Korean steel stocks after Seoul moved to impose anti-dumping duties on Chinese steel products.

South Korea's Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy late Thursday said that its trade commission had decided to ask the country's finance minister to impose as much as about 38% provisional anti-dumping duties on Chinese hot-rolled thick steel plates from next month.

Provisional duties of between 27.91% and 38.02% on Chinese steel products imported to South Korea would be needed to protect the potential damage to the local steel industry and the commission is launching a full state investigation into the alleged Chinese dumping, the ministry said.

An anti-dumping complaint by Hyundai Steel in July 2024 against Chinese rivals prompted the South Korean commission to launch a four-month preliminary probe starting in October.

The South Korean steel maker's stock climbed as much as 8.0% to an intraday high of 290,000 won, the equivalent of $202.42, on Friday. It was up 5.0% or more for most of the early trading session, outperforming the benchmark Kospi's 0.5% drop.

Smaller local steel companies Hyundai Steel and Dongguk Steel gained 4.1% and 7.6%, respectively, early Friday.

South Korean steelmakers are seen to be struggling with weak demand at home and abroad. The oversupply of steel products from China has aggravated the industry downturn, they say.

Posco Holdings posted a net loss for the final quarter of 2024, with its main steel business hit by lower product prices and higher operating costs amid weakening sales.

For the full year, the company's net profit nearly halved to 948.00 billion won and operating profit slumped 38% to 2.174 trillion won as revenue fell 5.8% to 72.688 trillion won.

Write to Kwanwoo Jun at kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com

data.title: China Could Survive Being Shut out of U.S. Entirely — Market Talk
data.description: Even a complete loss of access to the U.S. markets should be manageable over time for China, says Frederic Neumann, chief Asia economist at HSBC. However, a more material risk for China would be a tariff cascade, with other markets following the steps of the U.S. and imposing import restrictions on Chinese goods, he adds. Exports to the rest of the world make up 12.5% of China's GDP, a significantly larger share than those to the U.S., he adds. How might such a tariff cascade unfold? As Chinese exporters face rising barriers to accessing the U.S. market, they may divert shipments elsewhere. This could squeeze market share and margins of local producers, in turn raising demand for trade protection, he says. The risk of a tariff cascade cannot be ignored, Neumann adds. (james.glynn@wsj.com ; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

data.title: Class Action Filed Against Venture Global, Inc. (VG) Seeking Recovery for Investors - Contact Levi & Korsinsky
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Venture Global, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/venture-global-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=131114&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: This lawsuit is on behalf of all shareholders that purchased stock pursuant and/or traceable to Venture's registration statement for the initial public offering held on or about January 24, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: According to the complaint, Venture completed its initial public offering on January 27, 2025, selling 70 million shares at $24.00 per share. On February 5, 2025, TotalEnergies, an energy company that was a target customer of Venture, rejected opportunities to become a long-term customer of Venture, citing lack of trust. In particular, TotalEnergies CEO, stated that he was approached by Venture to see if the company would be interested in a long-term supply contract for liquefied natural gas from the Calcasieu Pass terminal in Louisiana, but he rejected the offer "because of what they are doing."

Venture is currently facing legal challenges from existing large clients, such as BP and Shell, due to delays in supply contracts as Venture commissions its projects. Given the fact that defendants ability to deliver liquefied natural gas (LNG) to the world and to continue development of Venture's five natural gas liquefication and export projects depends on customer contracts, defendants' failure to account for and address these issues caused statements in Venture's registration statement to be false and/or materially misleading at the time of the initial public offering.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Venture stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/venture-global-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=131114&wire=1 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. data.title: Shareholders of GSK plc Should Contact Levi & Korsinsky Before April 7, 2025 to Discuss Your Rights - GSK
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your GSK plc investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/gsk-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=131115&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against GSK plc that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between February 5, 2020 and August 14, 2022.

CASE DETAILS: According to the filed complaint, defendants represented to investors that GSK removed Zantac from the market "[b]ased on information available at the time and correspondence with regulators." GSK also stated that it was "continuing with investigations into the potential source of NDMA." Defendants also assured investors that "GSK, the FDA, and the EMA [European Medicines Agency] have all independently concluded that there is no evidence of a causal association between ranitidine therapy and the development of cancer in patients," findings that were "consistent with other ranitidine data published prior to 2019." Finally, defendants claimed that they could not "quantify or reliably estimate the liability." These representations were materially false or misleading. In truth, GSK was fully aware of the source of NDMA and had been for nearly 40 years before withdrawing Zantac from the market.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in GSK stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/gsk-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=131115&wire=1 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

data.title: NZ's Sky Network Television slips on HY loss, outlook downgrade
data.description:
** Shares of Sky Network Television fall 3.5% to NZ$2.51

** Stock fell 3.9% to NZ$2.5 in early trade, its largest intraday pct fall since mid-December

** The New Zealand-based media and broadcasting firm logs net loss NZ$1.8 mln ($1.04 mln) for HY vs profit NZ$29 mln last year

** Also sees FY25 NPAT in lower end of the NZ$35 mln-NZ$42.5 mln range, down from earlier forecast of NZ$40 mln-NZ$55 mln

** More than 199,000 shares change hands vs 30-day avg volume of around 123,000 shares

** Stock down 3.5% YTD, including current session's moves

($1 = 1.7352 New Zealand dollars)

data.title: Autohome Controlling Shareholder Relinquishes Stake; SVP Succeeds as CEO; Shares Fall 4%
data.description:
Autohome controlling shareholder Yun Chen Capital Cayman sold 41.91% of Autohome's shares to Cartech Holding for $1.8 billion, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong Exchange.

The stake held by Yun Chen, a subsidiary of Ping An Insurance , is equivalent to 200,884,012 shares in Autohome, the filing said.

Once the purchase by Cartech is completed, Yun Chen will hold 23,916,500 shares in Autohome, and will no longer be the controlling shareholder.

Cartech is a subsidiary of Haier Group.

Meanwhile Tao Wu stepped down as Chief Executive Officer effective Thursday, according to a separate filing.

Senior Vice President Song Yang succeeded Tao as CEO effective the same day.

Shares fell 4% during Friday's morning trading.

data.title: Rupee to benefit as dollar eases to year-to-date lows, eyes portfolio flows
data.description:
The Indian rupee is expected to open modestly stronger on Friday, benefiting from a broadly weaker dollar that was weighed down by positional adjustments as traders recalibrated their expectations about U.S. President Donald Trump's policies.

The 1-month non-deliverable forward indicated that the rupee will open at around 86.60 to the U.S. dollar compared with 86.66 in the previous session.

The dollar index declined to a more-than-two-month-low of 106.3 on Thursday and was last quoted a tad higher in Asia trading. The offshore Chinese yuan rose to a peak of 7.23, its highest level since late November, before trimming gains.

U.S. President Donald Trump's remark about a possible trade deal with China and a dip in U.S. bond yields have both weighed on the dollar, DBS Bank said in a note.

Comments from U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent that the administration had no plans to increase sales of long-dated debt weighed on U.S. bond yields on Thursday.

The rupee should benefit from a broadly softer dollar but could face resistance near 86.50, a trader at a mid-sized private bank said.

"On the flip side, state-run banks have been active around 86.90 levels and above (on USD/INR), which should keep a lid on gains," the trader added.

The local currency had climbed to a peak of 86.47 last week, largely on the back of heavy-handed intervention by the Reserve Bank of India.

While the prevailing bearish bias on the rupee has eased - as signalled by a reduction in short bets per a Reuters poll - portfolio outflows remain a sore point for the local currency.

Overseas investors have sold over $11 billion of local stocks over 2025 so far on a net basis. "If the dollar weakness persists, it may help ease the equity outflows and help out the rupee some more," a second trader at a private bank said.

KEY INDICATORS:

** One-month non-deliverable rupee (INRNDFOR=) forward at 86.78; onshore one-month forward premium at 18.25 paise

** Dollar index at 106.47

** Brent crude futures down 0% at $76.4 per barrel

** Ten-year U.S. note yield at 4.5%

** As per NSDL data, foreign investors bought a net $944.7 mln worth of Indian shares on Feb. 19

** NSDL data showed foreign investors sold a net $163.8 mln worth of Indian bonds on Feb. 19

data.title: China Resources Medical Expects 119% Surge in 2024 Profit; Shares Slide 4%
data.description:
China Resources Medical Holdings expects to report a profit attributable to owners of the parent of about 565 million yuan for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024, up 119% from an attributable profit of 258 million yuan a year earlier.

The company attributed the increase in profit to a 386 million yuan one-off impairment loss in 2023 related to Huai'an City Huaiyin Hospital, according to a Friday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The company plans to publish its annual results before the end of March.

The hospital business firm shares were down by 4% in early morning trading.

data.title: AustralianSuper fined $17 million for failing to merge member accounts
data.description:
AustralianSuper, the country's largest pension fund, was fined A$27 million ($17.28 million) following a court ruling that it had failed to merge multiple member accounts, Australia's corporate watchdog said in a statement on Friday.

($1 = 1.5627 Australian dollars)

data.title: British Business - February 21
data.description:
The following are the top stories on the business pages of British newspapers. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

The Times

-Britain is to offer European countries an "Australian-style" youth mobility scheme as part of British Prime Minister Keir Starmer's reset with Brussels.

-British chancellor Rachel Reeves has signalled that she could reform the savings regime and limit the amount that people can put in cash individual savings accounts amid calls from investment firms for a shake-up to boost Britain's faltering stock market.

The Guardian

-Britain's eurosceptic politician Nigel Farage contradicted Donald Trump, saying Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelenskiy "is not a dictator" after the U.S. President's attack on the Ukrainian leader this week.

-Britain's Pensions Minister Torsten Bells said that the crumbling public services are bad for business, and spending more taxpayers' money to fix them is a pro-growth policy.

The Telegraph

-Britain's Ministry of Defence (MoD) is hiring a diversity tsar with a "spiritual edge" even as British prime Minister Keir Starmer faces pressure to increase military spending amid looming threats posed by Russia.

-Amazon's MGM Studios will take creative control of the "James Bond" film franchise under a new joint venture with longtime rights holders Michael Wilson and Barbara Broccoli.

Sky News

-British and other European troops could be deployed to Ukrainian cities, ports and nuclear power plants to help secure the peace following any ceasefire deal with Russia.

-British Foreign Minister David Lammy said on Thursday he saw no appetite from Russia for peace with Ukraine after listening to his Russian counterpart Sergei Lavrov talk at a closed-door meeting of the top G20 diplomats in South Africa.

data.title: Fortescue's Fiscal First-Half Results Misses Expectations, Jarden Research Says
data.description:
Fortescue's fiscal first-half results were weak and missed expectations, driven by higher-than-expected depreciation and amortization (D&A) charges, according to a Thursday note by Jarden Research.

The company reported Thursday that its earnings per diluted share in the fiscal first half fell to $0.504 from $1.083 per diluted share a year earlier.

Revenue for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was $7.64 billion, down from $9.51 billion in the same period a year earlier.

The mining company's net profit after tax missed Jarden's estimates by 16%, while its underlying earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in 4% below expectations, while revenue also fell short on a like-for-like basis, Jarden noted.

However, Fortescue's reduced capital expenditure forecast for its energy division of $400 million reflects a shift in focus towards sustaining attractive yields, Jarden added.

Shares of the company rose 4% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Kamat Hotels Signs Management Pact for Upcoming Hotel in Mandavi, India
data.description:
Kamat Hotels (India) has signed a management agreement to manage and operate the 153-room Orchid Hotel at Mandavi in Kutch District of Gujarat, India.

The hotel is expected to start its operations by December 2027, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

data.title: Dollar Set for Third Consecutive Weekly Drop
data.description:
The dollar index held steady around 106.5 on Friday but remained on track for its third consecutive weekly decline, as President Donald Trump’s approach to tariffs has been less aggressive than markets initially feared.

Trump imposed a 10% additional tariff on Chinese goods within his first month in office, but so far, proposed tariffs on other countries and key sectors have remained only as threats.

This more cautious trade policy could alleviate concerns about inflationary pressures, providing the Federal Reserve with more room to cut interest rates further.

Meanwhile, minutes from the latest FOMC meeting reaffirmed the Fed's cautious approach, signaling no immediate urgency to ease policy amid an uncertain economic outlook.

Traders are now focused on Friday’s PMI readings and existing home sales data for further economic insights.

data.title: Peakstone Realty Trust reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Peakstone Realty Trust reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 35 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of -55 cents. The mean expectation of three analysts for the quarter was for a loss of 24 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from -39 cents to -2 cents per share.

  • Revenue fell 8.1% to $57.93 million from a year ago; analysts expected $54.68 million.

  • Peakstone Realty Trust's reported EPS for the quarter was 35 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $12.71 million.

  • Peakstone Realty Trust shares had fallen by 4.4% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 15.4% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The average consensus recommendation for the commercial reits peer group is also "hold"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Peakstone Realty Trust is $13.00

data.title: Iron Ore Rises Amid Tight Supply — Market Talk
data.description: Iron-ore prices are higher in early Asian trade amid tight supply. The Australian cyclone likely affected 7 million tons of iron-ore shipment, weighing on its supply, Nanhua Futures says in a research note. On the demand front, market sentiment is improving on the prospect of better macroeconomic performance in China, it adds. Investors will pay attention to China's National People's Congress in early March for any further property supportive measures. The most-traded iron ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is up 1.6% higher at 839.50 yuan a ton. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: COMMENT-USD/CNH bulls are long and wrong; Bessent may break them
data.description:
Sentiment for China's stock market, and the yuan, is shifting due to investor euphoria for Chinese AI companies and hints that Trump might forge a new trade deal. The outlook for USD/CNH is hence changing too; more longs may be forced to capitulate.

Thursday's dive through the 100-day moving average, currently at 7.2452, spooked USD-holders who unloaded to a low of 7.2314. While USD/CNH tentatively rebounded above that technical barrier early Friday, some longs are likely still caught and waiting for spikes to sell.

USD/CNH ebbed from its rebound high of 7.2553, last trading at 7.2473, as China stocks rally with Alibaba leading again, after its Q3 earnings beat. China's Premier Li Qiang on Thursday pledged more consumption-boosting measures soon, while the People's Bank of China vowed extra financial support for the private economy.

If USD/CNH ends Friday below the 100 DMA, the next support is between 7.2215-7.2222 where the 200 DMA meets the 38.2% Fibonacci retracement of the September-February rally to its record high. This key level should prove more resilient, but shorts will target it to break any lingering bullish convictions.

A potential catalyst for a breakdown to the 50% retracement at 7.1745 is U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's scheduled call with his Chinese counterpart on Friday. If there are hints of U.S.-China trade talks being planned, the yuan will rally again.

For more click on

data.title: Strong February Jobs Data Does Not Add to Reserve Bank of Australia's Concerns About Labor Tightness, Says ANZ Research
data.description:
Australia's jobs data for February does not add any risks to the Reserve Bank of Australia's (RBA) concerns about market labor market tightness despite an increase in employment, ANZ Research said in a Thursday note.

Official data showed on Feb. 20 that the unemployment rate rose to 4.1% in January, ahead of the 4% print in December 2024 and in ANZ Research's forecast. There were 44,000 additional jobs during the month, compared with 60,000 in December and ANZ's forecast for 10,000 new jobs.

While employment growth was higher than expected, it has not contributed to labor market tightening amid a record participation rate, per the note. ANZ Research added that wage growth slowed to 0.65% in the fourth quarter of 2024, its weakest since 2022.

"Our preferred measure of labor market tightness [has] been steady over recent months and remains at levels consistent with 2-3% inflation," ANZ Research wrote in its note.

In lowering the key rate last Feb. 18, the RBA said that despite a decline in inflation, "upside risks remain" with data "suggesting that the labor market may be somewhat tighter than previously thought."

data.title: Hong Kong Unemployment Stays at 3.1% in January Quarter
data.description:
Unemployment in Hong Kong was at 3.1% in the November 2024-January 2025 period, unchanged form the October to December 2024 period, according to data from the country's census and statistics department released Thursday.

Underemployment during the three-month period ended January stayed at 1.1%, the department said.

Total employment fell by 2,300 to 3.7 million in the January quarter from the December 2024 quarter, while the labor force slid by 5,700 to 3.8 million during the January quarter.

The number of unemployed persons slid by 3,500 to 110,100 in the January quarter compared with the December 2024 quarter.

Underemployed persons stayed at about 43,400 during the January quarter.

The labor market is seen to be largely stable in the near term but global economic uncertainties are seen to challenge businesses, Labor and Welfare Secretary Chris Sun said.

data.title: Bilibili's Hong Kong shares jump as Q4 profit beats estimates
data.description:
** Hong Kong shares of Chinese mobile games group Bilibili jump as much as 9.5% to HK$169.5, on course for the biggest one-day gain since February 14

** Stock on track to snap two consecutive session of fall; third-biggest gainer in Hang Seng Tech Index , which is up 3.5%

** U.S.-listed stock rose 8.8% to end at $22.11 on Thursday, their highest close since December 9

** Co reported Q4 adjusted EPS at 1.07 yuan ($0.15), above estimates of 0.95 yuan, and Q4 revenues from advertising were 2.39 billion yuan ($327.2 mln), up 24% YoY

** Citi maintains "Neutral/High Risk" rating on Bilibili amid weakening momentum over gaming and valuation being a bit stretched, while it lifts TP to $23 from $20.5 due to re-rating in the China internet sector and see ads growth as decent

** Hang Seng Commerce & Industry Index (.HSNC) rises 3.4%, Hang Seng Index adds 2.2%

** Bilibili Hong Kong stock up 18.2% YTD, U.S. shares up 22.1% YTD

Market Chatter: Trump Says New US-China Trade Deal Possible
data.description:
US President Donald Trump said a new trade deal between the US and China is possible, Reuters reported Wednesday.

Trump also said he expected Chinese President Xi Jinping to visit the US, but did not provide a date, according to the report.

Washington and Beijing have long had tense relations over trade, cybersecurity, TikTok, Taiwan, Hong Kong, human rights and COVID-19 origins, Reuters wrote.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Aluminum Edges Higher as EU Agrees on Fresh Russian Sanctions — Market Talk
data.description: Aluminum edges higher in early Asian trade on reports that the European Union has backed a ban on Russian aluminum, say ANZ research analysts in a commentary. EU Ambassadors have agreed to move forward with a fresh package of sanctions against the key supplier, the analysts say noting media reports. These include a gradual ban on imports, restrictions on dozens of Russian vessels and prohibiting about a dozen banks from accessing the SWIFT international payment system, the analysts add. The three-month LME Aluminum contract is 0.1% higher at $2,729.50 a ton. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Australia's Telix Pharmaceuticals scales record peak on upbeat revenue forecast
data.description:
Shares of Telix Pharmaceuticals surged to a record high on Friday after the company forecast annual bumper revenue, while its full-year earnings for 2024 soared more than ninefold.

The Australian biopharmaceutical firm's stock jumped as much as 15.3% by 0223 GMT, making it one of the top gainers in the benchmark S&P/ASX 200 index, which was down 0.2%.

Telix forecast on Thursday full-year 2025 revenue to be in the range of A$1.18 billion to A$1.23 billion ($755.55 million to $787.57 million), above consensus estimate of A$1.03 billion, according to analysts at TD Cowen.

($1 = 1.5618 Australian dollars)
